name: Rugalah
recipeInstructions:
The recipe I use that I got from Bon Appetit.
For Filling: Combine walnuts, raisins or currents, sugar and cinnamon in a small bowl and blend well. Set aside.
For Dough: Combine 3 1/2 cups flour with sugar, butter, eggs, honey, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl, adding more flour as necessary to make pliable dough.
Divide dough into thirds. Flatten into thin discs between sheets of waxed paper. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Grease and flour a baking sheet, shaking off excess flour.
Remove one batch of dough from the refrigerator. Roll out into a 12-inch circle and spread generously with melted butter (use about 2/3 cup).
Sprinkle with cinnamon mixture. Pat it into the dough.
Drizzle a little more butter over top.
Cut into 16 wedges using a pizza cutter or very sharp knife.
Roll each triangle up from the outside edge to the point. Arrange on the prepared baking sheet, point side down.
Bake until golden, about 15 - 18 minutes, watching carefully to prevent burning.
Repeat with remaining dough.
Store Rugelah in an airtight container.
